research.status_summary: McCann TLV is actively hiring (11+ open roles as of May 2026) and operationally stable, with no signals of layoffs or acquisition.
research.hiring_policy: All posted roles are Israel-based, full-time, and listed as hybrid (Tel Aviv District); no visa sponsorship or EOR-friendly remote policy is indicated — standard expectation is local Israeli presence.
recent_news: Won the Israel Electric Corporation (IEC) advertising account — a ₪40M/2-year budget — after IEC ended its decade-plus relationship with its previous agency (per mccann.co.il).

McCann TLV's CEO Amit Mashiah publicly discussed how AI will reshape the ad-agency labor market in a podcast appearance, signaling the agency is actively reckoning with AI integration at the leadership level.
The agency expanded its senior management team (six new managers placed across departments), with CEO Eti Avdi citing 'significant growth of the firm in the past year' as the driver.
research.growth_signal: With ~223–228 employees and $40.5M estimated annual revenue (RocketReach 2026), McCann TLV is in a modest growth trajectory, landing major new accounts (IEC, continued El Al at ~₪50M/year) and expanding headcount.
research.fit_for_lior: McCann TLV's explicit AI-in-advertising narrative and its Creative/Strategy/Digital departments offer a realistic entry point for a Creative AI or Prompt-to-Campaign product role, but as a traditional Israeli ad agency it's better suited to Lior's 'Creative AI' thread than her Product Analyst or Vibe Coder tracks.
